description_long: This is a futuristic, science-fiction inspired airplane with a sleek, elongated fuselage and a forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit featuring a dark canopy. The wings are straight and extend laterally from the midsection, each equipped with long, slender laser-like appendages at the tips. The rear of the airplane houses four large cylindrical jet engines, arranged in pairs, with visible exhaust ports. The surface is predominantly metallic gray with weathered, battle-worn textures and subtle panel lines, giving it a rugged, utilitarian appearance. The landing gear is minimal and appears to be a single strut under the nose, suggesting a retractable or specialized configuration. The overall design is reminiscent of a starfighter, blending advanced aerodynamics with a robust, armored look.
description: A sleek, metallic, sci-fi airplane with a forward cockpit, mid-mounted straight wings, and four large rear jet engines, featuring a weathered, battle-worn finish.
description_view_0: Side view showing the elongated fuselage, forward cockpit, and the profile of the four rear jet engines.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the pointed nose, cockpit canopy, and the full wingspan with laser-like tips.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the engines and wing-mounted appendages.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the four large jet engine exhausts and the full width of the wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight wings and extended laser-like appendages at the tips; no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Quad jet engines mounted at the rear fuselage in two pairs.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Single nose strut visible, suggesting a retractable tricycle configuration; main gear not visible.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single canopy, likely single-seat based on size and design.
